title: Vietnam Tech Summit 2026 to Bring AI Leaders, Founders, Investors, and Vietnamese Tech Talent to Silicon Valley
author: Vietspark
description: Vietnam Tech Summit 2026, the largest Vietnamese-led tech summit in the United States, will take place August 7-8 at the Computer History Museum in Mountain View, California. The fifth annual summit has grown from 100 attendees in 2022 to over 900 in 2025, featuring company tours, keynotes on AI, recruiting opportunities, mentorship, and the Vietnam AI Stars startup competition. The event aims to help Vietnamese and Vietnam-connected tech talent build careers in the AI era.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google is prominently featured as a keynote speaker (Quoc Le, Google Fellow and VP at Google DeepMind), company tour host, and mentoring floor participant, indicating strong engagement with the summit and Vietnamese tech talent development.

title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Intuit, Inc. - INTU
author: Pomerantz Llp
description: Pomerantz LLP is investigating Intuit for potential securities fraud following the company's May 20, 2026 announcement of disappointing fiscal Q3 2026 results. Intuit reported underperformance during tax season, facing pressure from price-sensitive DIY filers and losing market share on pricing. The company projected only 2% growth in TurboTax online paying units amid an industry-wide contraction. The stock fell 20.02% ($76.86 per share) to $307.07 on May 21, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Intuit faces a securities fraud investigation following disappointing Q3 2026 tax season results, significant revenue underperformance, loss of market share to price-sensitive competitors, and a substantial 20% stock price decline. The company also reported the most significant industry-wide contraction since the post-COVID period.

title: Identifying a Bullish Reversal Area for Salesforce Stock
author: Aleksandar Vichev
description: Salesforce stock has crashed 59% from its late-2024 peak of $369 to $150, despite healthy business growth and a single-digit P/E ratio. Using Elliott Wave analysis, the author suggests the stock is likely to fall further to around $100 before a bullish reversal occurs, as the current decline is part of a larger A-B-C flat correction pattern.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The stock has declined 59% from its peak and is expected to fall further to around $100 according to the Elliott Wave analysis presented. The article identifies an ongoing downtrend with no immediate bottom in sight, despite the company's strong fundamentals and attractive valuation metrics.
Mentioned only as a comparison point for contrarian bullish views on software companies; no specific analysis or sentiment is provided about the company itself.

title: $INTU Stock Notification: Lose Money on Your Intuit Investment? Contact BFA Law about its Ongoing Securities Fraud Investigation
author: Bfa Law
description: BFA Law is investigating Intuit for potential securities fraud related to misrepresentations about TurboTax's price positioning. The company's stock dropped 20% on May 20, 2026, after revealing it faced unexpected pressure from price-sensitive DIY tax filers and lost on pricing competitiveness during the 2026 tax season, contrary to prior statements about being positioned at the lowest price.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company faces securities fraud investigation due to alleged misrepresentations about TurboTax's competitive pricing. Stock declined 20% after disappointing 2026 tax season results, revealing the company lost market share among price-sensitive customers contrary to prior guidance about being positioned at the lowest price.

title: FactSet Stock Is Starting to Look Irresistible
author: Aleksandar Vichev
description: FactSet stock has plummeted 63% from its $500 peak in late 2024 to $185 in February 2026 due to market concerns about AI chatbots replacing financial data services. However, the author argues this sell-off is unjustified, as AI tools require quality data to be useful. Using Elliott Wave analysis, the author suggests the stock may decline further to $170 before becoming attractive, but views it as fundamentally undervalued even at current levels.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite the significant stock decline, the author argues the sell-off is unjustified and the company is fundamentally attractive. The author believes AI chatbots require quality financial data to function, making FactSet's core business essential. The stock is viewed as undervalued even at current prices and 'irresistible' below $180.

The author mentions Intuit as another software company that was unfairly sold off due to AI hype concerns, suggesting the market's pessimism toward software companies in the AI era is overblown and likely to reverse.
